name: ALZHEIMER DISEASE
definition: diffuse gray matter disease with large loss of cells from cerebral cortex + other areas

demographics.Incidence: 10% of people > 65 years of age; 50% of people > 85 years of age
demographics.Histo: intraneuronal deposits of abnormally phosphorylated τ protein (neurofibrillary tangles)
demographics.Location: early neuronal loss + gliosis in mesiotemporal cortex

dominant_finding: focal atrophic change in medial temporal lobe (82% sensitive, 75% specific, 80% accurate):
distribution: early neuronal loss + gliosis in mesiotemporal cortex
discriminating_features: focal atrophic change in medial temporal lobe (82% sensitive, 75% specific, 80% accurate):
classic pattern of altered cortical metabolism:
"cracked walnut" appearance of brain atrophy = symmetrically enlarged sulci in high-
volume loss of hippocampus + parahippocampal gyrus

general: "cracked walnut" appearance of brain atrophy = symmetrically enlarged sulci in high-
focal atrophic change in medial temporal lobe (82% sensitive, 75% specific, 80% accurate):
volume loss of hippocampus + parahippocampal gyrus
classic pattern of altered cortical metabolism:
hypometabolism in posterior cingulate gyrus, precuneus, posterior temporal + parietal
hypometabolism of prefrontal association cortices ± frontal lobe involvement
sparing of sensorimotor cortex, visual cortex, anterior cingulate cortex, basal ganglia,

clinical.Prognosis: 4th leading cause of death in individuals > 65 years
